⚒️ Article: Fern savannahs especially susceptible to wildfires spread rapidly following the collapse of forest ecosystem during the end-Triassic mass extinction

www.nature.com/articles/s41...

posts.external_description: Fern savannahs spread rapidly following the collapse of the forest ecosystem during the end-Triassic mass extinction, setting the conditions for widespread wildfires, according to an analysis of the d...

posts.external_description: This analysis shows that a widely used satellite-based ecosystem resilience indicator is a real signal linked to plant physiological responses to water deficit, provided that the estimation is based o...

posts.text:
Registration and call for abstracts for the BES Annual Meeting 2026 are now open 🔥

Our sold-out and Europe's largest ecology conference is returning this year to Birmingham's ICC on 14–17 December, and we can't wait to see you there.

Find out more here 👇
https://tinyurl.com/2s3nupvu

Nature research paper: Rising dust pollution across Europe in a changing climate

go.nature.com/4wO1Dnj
posts.external_title: Rising dust pollution across Europe in a changing climate - Nature
posts.external_description: A database of daily dust metals in Europe is established and a machine learning model shows that transported dust intrusion events over the past decade are linked to changes in atmospheric circulation, while an ice core shows that the increase in dust since pre-industrial times is governed by increasing aridity in North Africa..

posts.text:
🚨🚨We have a new paper out in @natgeosci.nature.com led by postdoctoral scholar Na Wang (now  @ucsantabarbara.bsky.social), where we identify a last-millennium climate “seesaw” linking extreme #hydroclimate events across both Central Asia & mainland Southeast Asia ☔️ 

www.nature.com/articles/s41...
posts.external_title: Opposing hydroclimate extremes in Central and mainland Southeast Asia over past millennium - Nature Geoscience
posts.external_description: Opposing wet and dry conditions in Central and mainland Southeast Asia over the past millennium were controlled by climate variability over the Pacific and Indian oceans, a pattern likely to become mo...

posts.author_name: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
posts.text: Some Indonesian palm oil companies have made zero-deforestation promises, and most have maintained less than 1% deforestation per year. A study finds that this was more likely due to broad economic and policy conditions than to restraint. In PNAS: https://ow.ly/sGkx50Zoa4b

posts.author_name: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
posts.text: Opinion piece: #EnhancedRockWeathering removes #CO2 from the atmosphere. But the practice might actually have more promise as a #farming practice than as a #CarbonDioxideRemoval technology. In PNAS Front Matter: https://ow.ly/ekUM50Zo5hp
